steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence opens with "Initially overlooked as insignificant research by the scientific community" — a modifier that must attach to the subject immediately following the comma. Rule: the noun right after the comma must be the thing that was overlooked. What was overlooked? Research — so the subject must be a piece of research, not a person.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. Milstein studied monoclonal antibodies in 1975; they — subject after the comma is "Milstein" (a person). Reconstruct: "Initially overlooked as insignificant research, Milstein studied..." — this says Milstein was overlooked as research, which is illogical. Eliminated.

B. in 1975 Milstein studied monoclonal antibodies, which — subject after the comma is "Milstein" again. Same dangling modifier problem: Milstein was not research. Eliminated.

C. Milstein's 1975 study of monoclonal antibodies — subject after the comma is "Milstein's 1975 study" — a study is research. Reconstruct: "Initially overlooked as insignificant research, Milstein's 1975 study of monoclonal antibodies contributed to..." — the modifier attaches correctly. Correct.

D. monoclonal antibodies were studied by Milstein in 1975 and — subject after the comma is "monoclonal antibodies." Reconstruct: "Initially overlooked as insignificant research, monoclonal antibodies were studied..." — antibodies are not research; they are the subject of the research. Eliminated.
